welcome_pitch: We already have everything we need. We just lack the means to exchange it.
welcome_cycle_result: Nobody paid a euro. Everyone got what they needed.
consent_no_money_title: This records mutual debts, not money
consent_no_money_body: When someone sends you credit they are saying "I owe you." No real euros are transferred.
consent_minimal_data_title: We store almost nothing about you
consent_minimal_data_body: Only your phone number and a display name. No payment details needed.
consent_balance_visible_title: Other members can see your balance
consent_balance_visible_body: Before accepting credit from you, the other person can see your balance. This helps them verify your credibility.
consent_open_source_body: This app is free and open. Anyone can inspect how it works, and any community can run their own version.
phone_body: We'll send you a one-time code. No password needed, ever.
phone_hint: Used only for sign-in. Never shared.
email_body: We'll send you a one-time code.
email_hint: Used only for sign-in. Never shared.
otp_sent_to: Sent to {destination}. Should arrive within a few seconds.
verified_body: Your number is confirmed. Let's get you set up.
intro1_body1: When someone sends you credit, they are saying: I owe you. When you send credit, you are saying: I owe you. No real money moves.
intro1_body2: Because credit has a positive side and a negative side that cancel out, the community can trade to the extent of its trust — not to the extent of its cash. You effectively create liquidity together, from nothing.
intro2_body1: Your balance shows what the community owes you (positive) or what you owe the community (negative).
intro2_body2: Being negative just means you've received more than you've given so far. The community trusts you to contribute over time.
intro3_body: This is how other members will see you. You can change it later in Settings.
home_balance_first_use: Your balance starts at zero. Send or receive credit to get started.
home_empty_state:
Your balance starts at zero.
Tap Send or Receive to get started.
send_consent_body1: When you send credit to someone, you are lending them value. You trust that they will contribute back to the community over time — directly to you, or to others.
send_consent_body2: There is no automatic repayment. If you have agreed on specific conditions — a timeframe, a service in return, anything — write them in the description when you send. That note belongs to the transaction record.
send_consent_body3: The system works best when people do not demand repayment. But you always have the right to ask.
send_qr_caption: Show this to the other person. They scan it to accept.
cancel_confirm_body: The QR code will be invalidated and can no longer be used.
receive_helper: Enter the amount you'd like to request.
receive_qr_caption: Ask the other person to scan this to send you credit.
accept_send_balance_impact: Your balance will increase by {amount} and {name}'s will decrease by {amount}.
accept_receive_balance_impact: Your balance will decrease by {amount} and {name}'s will increase by {amount}.
accept_first_time_notice: Accepting records a community credit — not real money. Balances will update in your shared balance.
accept_expired: This link has expired or is invalid.
accept_sign_in_required: Sign in to accept this transaction.
scan_no_camera: No camera found on this device
scan_permission_denied: Camera access was denied. Please allow camera access in your browser settings.
scan_invalid_qr: Not a valid Mutuvia QR code
about_description: {appName} is a mutual credit ledger for communities. No real money moves — credit records mutual obligations between members.
about_with_ai: with Claude (Anthropic's AI assistant) as a coding collaborator
faq_a_what_is_mutual_credit: Mutual credit is a way for a community to exchange without using money. When you send someone credit, you are saying: I owe you. When you receive credit, someone is saying they owe you. Nothing real changes hands — it is a shared record of who owes what to whom.
faq_a_is_this_real_money: No. Nothing in this app is real money. Credits are a record of mutual obligations between community members. Think of it as a shared notebook where we keep track of favours.
faq_q_negative_balance: What does a negative balance mean? Is that bad?
faq_a_negative_balance: A negative balance means you have received more from the community than you have given so far. That is completely normal — it just means the community trusts you to contribute over time. There is no interest, no deadline, and no penalty.
faq_q_someone_doesnt_give_back: What happens if someone does not give back?
faq_a_someone_doesnt_give_back: The system relies on trust and social bonds. If someone consistently takes without giving, the community can see their balance and decide whether to keep extending credit. Based on community feedback, we are also exploring various conflict resolution and feedback mechanisms.
faq_q_who_sees_my_data: Who can see my transactions and balance?
faq_a_who_sees_my_data: Before someone accepts credit from you, they can see your current balance — this helps them decide whether to trust you. Your full transaction history is visible only to you.
faq_q_how_to_send_receive: How do I send or receive credit?
faq_a_how_to_send_receive: Tap Send or Receive on the home screen, enter an amount and an optional note, then show the QR code to the other person. They scan it to complete the exchange.
faq_q_what_is_the_note: What is the note field for?
faq_a_what_is_the_note: The note is an optional description you can attach to any transaction. Use it to record what the credit is for, or any conditions you have agreed on — a timeframe, a service in return, anything. The note stays with the transaction permanently.
faq_a_can_i_trust_this: All data is processed and stored within the EU. The app is fully open source, so anyone can read the code, verify what it does, or run their own instance.
faq_a_is_open_source: Yes. The code is licensed under AGPL-3.0. Any community can read it, audit it, or run their own instance. Changes that affect your data or credits are always announced in advance.
faq_a_who_made_this: This app was built by Mathijs de Bruin, with Claude (Anthropic's AI assistant) as a coding collaborator.
faq_q_question_not_answered: My question is not answered here.
faq_a_question_not_answered: We would love to hear from you. You can ask a question, report a bug, or suggest a feature on GitHub.
faq_link_report_issue: Open an issue or discussion on GitHub
error_not_found_body: We couldn't find this page. It may have moved or no longer exists.
error_server_body: We've been notified and are looking into it. Please try again.
install_banner_body: Add to your home screen for the best experience.
install_banner_ios_hint: Tap the share button, then "Add to Home Screen".
